evals.prompt: Add a new action to my App Builder project that calls the AEM Assets API using IMS service-to-service authentication. It should list all assets in a given folder path.
evals.expected_output: Action created with proper IMS auth setup, correct manifest entry in ext.config.yaml, Adobe API call implementation, and error handling.

assertions.description: Creates a new Runtime action source file.
Implements the outbound HTTP call used to reach the AEM Assets API.
Registers the new action in the extension manifest.
Builds the updated App Builder project successfully.

evals.prompt: My Runtime action keeps timing out when processing large files. It currently has the default 60-second timeout. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of timeout limits (60s web default, 300s non-web max), manifest configuration to increase timeout, and suggestions for async processing patterns.

assertions.description: Raises the action timeout above the 60-second default in manifest configuration.
Explains the default 60-second web action timeout.
Explains the 300-second maximum for non-web actions.

evals.prompt: Create a scheduled action that runs every hour to sync inventory data from a third-party REST API to Adobe Commerce, using State SDK to track the last sync timestamp.
evals.expected_output: Cron-style action with Alarm trigger, State SDK for persistence, HTTP client for REST calls, error handling, and proper manifest configuration.

assertions.description: Creates the scheduled sync action source file.
Uses the App Builder State SDK to persist sync state.
Configures an Alarm trigger/feed for scheduled execution.
Builds the scheduled sync project successfully.

evals.prompt: I need a web action that accepts multipart file uploads from a React frontend and stores the files using the Files SDK. Files can be up to 5MB.
evals.expected_output: Raw web action for binary input handling, Files SDK storage implementation, size validation (within 1MB web limit guidance), and suggestions for handling larger files.

assertions.description: Creates the upload action source file.
Configures the upload action as a raw web action for binary payloads.
Uses the App Builder Files SDK to store uploaded files.
Builds the file-upload project successfully.

evals.prompt: Create an action that stores and retrieves product catalog data using the App Builder Database SDK. It should support listing products with pagination, adding new products, and updating product details.
evals.expected_output: Database-backed product catalog action with App Builder Database SDK usage, list/create/update handlers, pagination support, manifest registration, and a buildable project.

assertions.description: Creates a new Runtime action source file for the product catalog workflow.
Uses the App Builder Database SDK in the product catalog action.
Registers the product catalog action in the extension manifest.
Builds the database CRUD action project successfully.

evals.prompt: Set up a Runtime action as an Adobe I/O Events webhook receiver. It should handle the challenge verification request and process incoming AEM asset modification events.
evals.expected_output: Webhook-capable Runtime action with challenge verification handling, POST event processing for incoming AEM asset modification events, and manifest registration.

assertions.description: Handles the Adobe I/O Events challenge verification request.
Processes POST webhook payloads for asset modification events.
Registers the webhook receiver action in the extension manifest.

evals.prompt: Create an action that publishes custom events when a user completes a purchase. The action should use the Events SDK to fire an event with the order details.
evals.expected_output: Runtime action that uses the Events SDK to publish a purchase-completed custom event with order details and includes the necessary event-publishing logic.

assertions.description: Uses the Adobe I/O Events SDK in the custom event publisher action.
Publishes a custom event with the order payload.

evals.prompt: Build a cron action that polls the Adobe I/O Events Journaling API every 5 minutes to consume asset upload events. It should track its position using State SDK so no events are lost.
evals.expected_output: Scheduled journaling consumer action with five-minute trigger configuration, State SDK position tracking, Adobe I/O Events journaling consumption, and loss-resistant event processing.

assertions.description: Uses the App Builder State SDK to persist the journaling cursor.
Implements Adobe I/O Events journaling consumption logic.
Configures a scheduled trigger/feed for the journaling consumer.

evals.prompt: My action needs to return a large JSON report (5MB+). Create an action that writes the report to Files SDK storage and returns a redirect to a presigned download URL.
evals.expected_output: Runtime action that writes a large report to Files SDK storage, creates a presigned download URL, and returns an HTTP redirect response to that URL.

assertions.description: Uses the App Builder Files SDK for large report storage.
Generates a presigned URL for downloading the large report.
Returns a redirect response instead of the large payload inline.

evals.prompt: Create a two-action sequence: the first action validates and enriches incoming order data, the second action stores it in the database. Configure them as a sequence in the manifest.
evals.expected_output: Two separate Runtime actions for order validation/enrichment and database storage, plus manifest sequence configuration chaining them together.

assertions.description: Configures the composed actions as a sequence in the extension manifest.
Creates the first action that validates and enriches order data.
Creates the second action that stores order data.

evals.prompt: Create a custom Asset Compute worker that generates a grayscale rendition of uploaded images for AEM Assets.
evals.expected_output: Asset Compute worker implementation that uses the Asset Compute SDK, generates a grayscale rendition for uploaded images, and includes rendition callback handling.

assertions.description: Uses the Asset Compute SDK in the custom worker.
Implements grayscale rendition handling for Asset Compute.
